Source: Multi-Sector Credit Asset Allocation Perspectives: Defensive carry amid asymmetric risks
Explanation: Two views for this asset disagreed and could not be reconciled automatically, so neither was kept. A human should decide which is correct.
Evidence / notes: multiple views survived validation for the same source/group and leaf; arbiter: These six candidates are not restatements of one stance but genuinely distinct instruments (RMBS, ABS, CLO-AAA, CLO-BBB, and a general securitized-vs-corporates preference) bundled into a single taxonomy leaf, and their published levels genuinely disagree in sign, not just intensity: RMBS (+1) and ABS (+1) are explicit positive dials, the general commentary favors securitized credit broadly, but the CLO section's own printed score nets to neutral (0) precisely because it is a two-sided path — AAA scored +0.5 against BBB scored -1 (rule: 'a two-sided path nets to N ... with both sides quoted'). No single candidate speaks for the whole combined leaf: picking any one O candidate would erase the explicit CLO-BBB caution the house itself prints, and picking the CLO-overall N would erase the equally explicit RMBS/ABS overweight scores on other components of the same leaf. This is rule 5's 'source genuinely holds both views at once' with no primary stance across the bundled components, so winning_index is null and the conflict should route to unresolved-conflict for analyst review.
